description: Bilingual Ayurveda Guṇa Matcher — students drag or tap materials onto Sanskrit/English guṇa-pair cards. Correct matches lock with reasoning; wrong matches return with a hint. A summary card shows all correspondences.

width.description: Max width of the tool container in pixels

themeColor.description: Primary brand color (deep purple per design system)

darkMode.description: Enable dark mode (not yet themed — reserved for future)

additionalProps.description: Override materials and guṇa pairs for custom lessons or curriculum variants.

materials.description: List of material tiles shown in the top row.

emoji.description: Single emoji for visual identity, e.g. '⚙️'

gunaPairs.description: Guṇa-pair cards on which materials are dropped.

correctMaterialId.description: Must match a material id above

default.hint: Think of something you cannot scratch with a fingernail.
default.reasoning: Iron exemplifies kaṭhina (hard) — it resists compression and cannot be easily scratched.

default.hint: Which material flows and takes the shape of its container?
default.reasoning: Water embodies khāla (moving) — it flows, ripples, and never holds a fixed form.

default.hint: Which material is airy, weighs very little, and can float in a breeze?
default.reasoning: Cloth is laghu (light) — woven fibres trap air, making it featherlight compared to metals.

default.hint: Think of something liquid yet thick, slow-flowing and viscous.
default.reasoning: Honey is sāndra (dense/viscous) — it flows but is thick, concentrated, and heavy for its volume.

default.hint: Which material can be carved, is firm yet workable, and has a natural grain?
default.reasoning: Wood is mṛdu (soft relative to iron) — it can be carved, sanded smooth, and shaped by hand.

default_5_materials.description: Standard 5-material, 5-pair game matching the NCERT Chapter 6 guṇas

extended_10_materials.description: Extended 10-material variant covering all 10 guṇa pairs from Aṣhtānga Hṛidaya
extended_10_materials.note: Add 5 more materials (fire, stone, oil, feather, ice) and 5 more pairs (uṣhṇa/hima, tīkṣhṇa/manda, rukṣha/snigdha, sthūla/sūkṣhma, picchhila/viśhada)

interactionModel.drag: HTML5 drag-and-drop — works on desktop. Material tile sets draggable=true; guṇa card has onDrop.
interactionModel.tap: Mobile-first tap-to-select: tap a material to select it (highlighted), then tap a guṇa pair to place it.

feedback.correct: Card locks with a ✓ badge, confetti emoji burst, and a bilingual reasoning box.
feedback.wrong: Card shakes (shakeX animation), hint appears below the drop zone.

interactionModel.summary: When all pairs are matched, an All Done banner appears with 'View Summary' button that opens a modal listing every material → guṇa reasoning.

whenToUse: Student is studying Ayurveda, guṇas, or ancient Indian classification of matter
Student is reading NCERT Science Grade 6 Chapter 6 (Materials Around Us)
Teacher wants an activity connecting Sanskrit vocabulary to physical properties
Student needs bilingual Sanskrit/English reinforcement of material properties

quickCall.title: Ayurveda Guṇa Matcher
quickCall.instructions_for_student: Drag each material (Iron, Water, Cloth, Honey, Wood) onto the guṇa pair that describes it best. Read the Sanskrit aloud as you go!
